---
title: "AI Literacy for Economists"
subtitle: "Modular lessons for teaching your students to use AI effectively"
page-layout: full
body-classes: landing-page
title-block-style: none
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<section class="landing-hero">
  <div class="landing-hero-inner">
    <div class="hero-label">Open Teaching Resource</div>
    <h1>AI Literacy<br>for Economists</h1>
    <p class="hero-subtitle">Modular lessons for teaching your students to use AI effectively — and to know when not to.</p>
    <p class="hero-credit">Curated by <a href="https://eabeam.github.io">Emily Beam</a> · University of Vermont</p>
  </div>
</section>

<hr class="dashed-divider">

<div class="how-strip">
  <div class="how-item">
    <span class="how-num">1</span>
    <div class="how-text"><strong>Pick a module</strong>Each one stands alone</div>
  </div>
  <div class="how-item">
    <span class="how-num">2</span>
    <div class="how-text"><strong>Adapt or use as-is</strong>CC-BY licensed</div>
  </div>
  <div class="how-item">
    <span class="how-num">3</span>
    <div class="how-text"><strong>Teach it</strong>30–75 min per module</div>
  </div>
</div>

<hr class="dashed-divider">
```

::: {.landing-content}

```{=html}
<!-- ──── Foundations ──── -->
<div class="track-section">
  <h2 class="track-title">
    Foundations
    <span class="track-label">No coding required</span>
  </h2>

  <div class="module-card">
    <div class="module-card-body">
      <a href="modules/a1-what-llms-do.html">A1: What LLMs Actually Do</a>
      <p class="module-desc">Tokens, prediction, and mental models for economists · ~50 min</p>
    </div>
    <span class="module-arrow">→</span>
  </div>

  <div class="module-card">
    <div class="module-card-body">
      <a href="modules/a2-prompting.html">A2: Prompting as Problem Specification</a>
      <p class="module-desc">Clear prompts = clear thinking. The prompt is your identification strategy · ~50 min</p>
    </div>
    <span class="module-arrow">→</span>
  </div>

  <div class="module-card">
    <div class="module-card-body">
      <a href="modules/a3-when-to-use-ai.html">A3: When AI Helps vs. Hurts</a>
      <p class="module-desc">Metacognition for the AI era — when to use it, when to struggle · ~30 min</p>
    </div>
    <span class="module-arrow">→</span>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ──── Technical ──── -->
<div class="track-section">
  <h2 class="track-title">Technical Foundations</h2>

  <div class="module-card">
    <div class="module-card-body">
      <a href="modules/b1-terminal-basics.html">B1: Terminal Basics</a>
      <p class="module-desc">What is a shell, navigating files, basic commands, and why reproducibility matters · ~75 min</p>
    </div>
    <span class="module-arrow">→</span>
  </div>

  <div class="module-card">
    <div class="module-card-body">
      <a href="modules/b2-git-github.html">B2: Git &amp; GitHub Essentials</a>
      <p class="module-desc">Version control for economists — clone, commit, push, and why you should care · ~75 min</p>
    </div>
    <span class="module-arrow">→</span>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ──── Econ Workflows ──── -->
<div class="track-section">
  <h2 class="track-title">AI for Economics Workflows</h2>

  <div class="module-card">
    <div class="module-card-body">
      <a href="modules/c1-lit-review.html">C1: Literature Review &amp; Synthesis</a>
      <p class="module-desc">Using AI to survey a field — and catching what it gets wrong · ~50 min</p>
    </div>
    <span class="module-arrow">→</span>
  </div>

  <div class="module-card">
    <div class="module-card-body">
      <a href="modules/c2-code-assistance.html">C2: Code Assistance (Stata/R)</a>
      <p class="module-desc">Debugging, translation, documentation — and when AI code is dangerous · ~50 min</p>
    </div>
    <span class="module-arrow">→</span>
  </div>

  <div class="module-card">
    <div class="module-card-body">
      <a href="modules/c3-data-exploration.html">C3: Data Exploration &amp; Cleaning</a>
      <p class="module-desc">Describing datasets, flagging anomalies — and why cleaning decisions are yours · ~50 min</p>
    </div>
    <span class="module-arrow">→</span>
  </div>

  <div class="module-card">
    <div class="module-card-body">
      <a href="modules/c4-writing-revision.html">C4: Writing &amp; Revision</a>
      <p class="module-desc">AI as copy editor, not ghostwriter — maintaining your voice · ~50 min</p>
    </div>
    <span class="module-arrow">→</span>
  </div>

  <div class="module-card">
    <div class="module-card-body">
      <a href="modules/c5-math-derivations.html">C5: Math &amp; Derivations</a>
      <p class="module-desc">Checking proofs, working through optimization — and verifying every step · ~50 min</p>
    </div>
    <span class="module-arrow">→</span>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ──── Faculty Quick Start ──── -->
<div class="track-section">
  <h2 class="track-title">
    Faculty Quick Start
    <span class="track-label">App-based, no terminal</span>
  </h2>

  <div class="module-card">
    <div class="module-card-body">
      <a href="modules/f1-first-real-task.html">F1: Your First Real Task</a>
      <p class="module-desc">Stop copy-pasting — point the app at your files and see the difference · ~15 min</p>
    </div>
    <span class="module-arrow">→</span>
  </div>

  <div class="module-card">
    <div class="module-card-body">
      <a href="modules/f2-course-prep.html">F2: Course Prep Power Hour</a>
      <p class="module-desc">Turn last semester's materials into working drafts in minutes · ~25 min</p>
    </div>
    <span class="module-arrow">→</span>
  </div>

  <div class="module-card">
    <div class="module-card-body">
      <a href="modules/f3-feedback-at-scale.html">F3: Feedback at Scale</a>
      <p class="module-desc">Better feedback, less grading dread — scaffold, don't automate · ~20 min</p>
    </div>
    <span class="module-arrow">→</span>
  </div>

  <div class="module-card">
    <div class="module-card-body">
      <a href="modules/f4-course-ai-ready.html">F4: Make Your Course AI-Ready</a>
      <p class="module-desc">Use AI to audit your syllabus, test assignments, and draft policy · ~25 min</p>
    </div>
    <span class="module-arrow">→</span>
  </div>

  <div class="also-see">
    <a href="modules/terminal-transition.html">When to Move to the Terminal →</a>
    <a href="modules/resources.html">Resources →</a>
  </div>
</div>

<hr class="section-divider">
```

## Design Philosophy

1. **Each module stands alone.** No prerequisites, no required sequence.
2. **Economics-native examples.** Not "write a poem" — "debug this regression," "summarize this literature."
3. **Honest about limitations.** We teach when AI fails, not just when it shines.
4. **Platform-flexible.** Concepts transfer across tools. UVM-specific notes where relevant.

## For Instructors

Every module includes:

- **Learning objectives** aligned to AI literacy competencies
- **Suggested timing** and natural break points
- **Discussion questions** and reflection prompts
- **Adaptation notes** for different course contexts

Licensed [CC-BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/) — use freely with attribution.

## About

Created by [Emily Beam](https://eabeam.github.io), Department of Economics, University of Vermont.

Built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org). Source on [GitHub](https://github.com/eabeam/teaching-ai).

:::
